title: Smoky Roasted Kiswahili Tomato Stir-fry
description: A savory and crispy vegetarian-friendly dish that features clotted cream, smoke, and is reminiscent of a romantic Kenyan cuisine.
instructions: Trim the stems from the tomatoes and cut them into quarters.
Mix together olive oil, garlic, red pepper flakes, salt, thyme leaves, and smoked paprika in a large bowl. Add the tomato quarters and toss until evenly coated with the marinade.
Place the marinated tomatoes on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Roast for 25-30 minutes or until the tomatoes are slightly caramelized and golden brown.
While the tomatoes are roasting, heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Add clotted cream to the skillet and let it simmer for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until reduced by half.
Remove the roasted tomatoes from the oven and drizzle with the reduced clotted cream mixture. Serve hot as a side dish or alongside rice.
